title: Tamil Kara Kuzhambu

subs.name: 2 drumstick pods, cut in 5 cm pieces

ingredients.name: tamarind
visual_checks: soak in 200 ml warm water, extract pulp
ingredients.notes: soak in 200 ml warm water, extract pulp

subs.name: 3 tbsp coconut milk powder blended with water to a thick paste
coconut oil for a milder flavour

steps.text: Soak tamarind in 200 ml warm water for 10 minutes and extract the pulp. Grind the grated coconut with the red chilli powder and coriander powder and just enough water to make a smooth, thick paste in a mixie.

Heat gingelly oil in a kadai over medium-high flame. Splutter mustard seeds, add fenugreek seeds and fry 15 seconds. Add curry leaves, then the shallots and cook until they begin to caramelise, about 6 minutes.
Add the tomato and cook until it softens completely. Add the brinjal pieces and fry for 4 minutes, turning them so all sides get some colour and the brinjal begins to soften.

Add turmeric and stir for 20 seconds. Pour in the tamarind extract, add salt, and bring to a boil. Simmer on medium for 12 minutes until the raw tamarind smell cooks away and the gravy reduces slightly.

Stir in the coconut-spice paste and simmer for another 8 minutes until the oil rises to the surface and the gravy turns deep red and glossy. Taste — the kuzhambu should be bold and spicy with a pronounced sourness.
Remove from flame and rest 5 minutes before serving. Serve with steamed rice and a cooling side of curd or raita; the kuzhambu improves in flavour by the next day.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: brinjal → 2 drumstick pods, cut in 5 cm pieces; coconut → 3 tbsp coconut milk powder blended with water to a thick paste; gingelly-oil → coconut oil for a milder flavour; shallot → 1 medium onion, roughly chopped
